_comment: On This Day (Historical Reenactment) campaign import for YakYak — structure mirrors a real /workflow/export-campaign export. This is docs/alternative_setups.md #7, a 'Computed (date) + Extracted (public-domain history)' show. Config + a SINGLE recurring host in the cast roster (The Chronicler, a time-traveling narrator who opens and closes every episode); no rendered assets (those generate per episode). Import: POST /workflow/import-campaign with body { userId, importData: <this file> }, or just run marketing/showrunner/setup_show.sh which does it and writes CAMPAIGN_ID into show.env. The style resolves by label ('Photorealism'); no style.id is set because style uuids are per-environment. The sepia-archival-turning-to-color documentary art direction lives in movies[0].style (appended to every image prompt). CRITICAL DIFFERENCE from the other showrunner shows: allowNewCharacters is TRUE — the daily historical figures (Nero, Lincoln, the barons at Runnymede, …) are different every episode and are introduced per-episode from the scene briefs by gen-movie-screenplay; only The Chronicler is a fixed recurring character, providing brand continuity (the doc's 'anchor + rotating guests' lifecycle). The story comes from a baked public-domain corpus (corpus/on_this_day.md) keyed by today's calendar date by compute.js, then dramatized via `claude -p` (REQUIRES_MODEL=true). aspectRatio is '16:9' because history performs on YouTube (the doc calls for 16:9 + 9:16; 16:9 is the primary cut). animationType is 'kenburns' on purpose — period imagery drifting under Ken Burns nails the documentary feel; Kling AI is the doc's #7 target for the dramatic centerpiece once it's reliable. The single movie is a season=null/episode=null TEMPLATE: copyCastFromSource clones The Chronicler into each generated episode.
message: Hand-authored On This Day cast/config template (no rendered assets)

campaigns.name: On This Day — History, Reenacted
campaigns.prompt: A daily history show: each episode takes one real anniversary tied to today's calendar date and stages it as a dramatized reenactment — The Chronicler, a time-traveling narrator, frames the event; the historical figures of that day live it out. Seven scenes, ending on the 'On this day…' takeaway as a caption card. Different historical cast every day, unified by the recurring host.

style.label: Photorealism
style.description: Photorealistic historical-documentary look — period-accurate, cinematic, archival texture resolving into rich color.

movies.title: On This Day
movies.plot: Recurring host template for the daily On This Day history show. Holds only The Chronicler (the time-traveling narrator who opens + closes every episode); the day's historical figures are introduced per episode from the scene briefs (allowNewCharacters). Each episode clones this template.
movies.style: Cinematic historical-documentary photorealism; period-accurate costume, architecture and props for the era depicted; the look of aged archival photography — warm sepia tone, fine film grain, dust and soft vignette — resolving into rich, saturated cinematic color on the dramatic beats; dramatic naturalistic lighting, shallow depth of field, a single human figure or moment held in a film-still composition, museum-grade gravitas.

customCast.name: The Chronicler
customCast.description: The Chronicler: the recurring host of On This Day — a calm, erudite time-traveling narrator, an ageless figure in a long dark travel-worn coat over period-neutral clothing, a brass pocket-watch and a worn leather chronicle in hand, kind knowing eyes, faint sepia light and drifting archival dust around them as if standing between eras, photorealistic cinematic-documentary style.

cast.name: The Chronicler
cast.description: The Chronicler — time-traveling narrator who frames each anniversary; opens & closes every episode. The one fixed recurring character; the day's historical figures change every episode.
